creator: freebsd@kumba.dev
text:
Yup, works fine on two Protectli appliances I have that use an I211 ethernet chipset (VP2410, FW6E).  dmesg(8) shows this for each port:

> igb0: <Intel(R) PRO/1000 Network Connection, Version - 2.5.31> port 0xe000-0xe01f mem 0xa1300000-0xa131ffff,0xa1320000-0xa1323fff irq 22 at device 0.0 on pci1
> igb0: Using MSIX interrupts with 3 vectors
> igb0: Ethernet address: xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx
> igb0: Bound queue 0 to cpu 0
> igb0: Bound queue 1 to cpu 1

As for the maintainer question, I did originally submit the port (just copied intel-em-kmod and changed a few variables, IIRC), but I think I left the Intel team as the port owner.  I check for updates by first checking the link in [1] and clicking the drop-down, then looking for new versions (the ordering inside the drop-down may not be numerically sorted right).

If I do not see a new version there, then I download the latest Intel Ethernet Adapter Complete Driver Pack at [2], unpack it, and check the PRO* directories for a "FreeBSD" directory, and if there is one, check the driver versions inside of it.  Sometimes updates for FreeBSD/Intel drivers show up in this kit a few months before someone at Intel updates the link in [1].

1. https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/download/14610/822716/intel-network-adapter-driver-for-82575-6-and-82580-based-gigabit-network-connections-under-freebsd.html

2. https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/download/15084/intel-ethernet-adapter-complete-driver-pack.html
